species: Ca. Methylomirabilis lanthanidiphila (bacterial anaerobic methanotroph, n-damo)

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Methanoperedenaceae archaea: a 20-year research journey. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-69699-0. [PMC13046784]
growth_records.snippet: bacterial anaerobic methanotrophs ("Ca. Methylomirabilis lanthanidiphila"), originally estimated to double every 2-8 weeks, have seen this reduced to just 5 days in a recent study
growth_records.notes: Review-cited secondary value, not a primary measurement of this paper. Recent doubling time = 5 days => td = 5*24 = 120 h. mu = ln2/120 = 0.693147/120 = 0.005776 /h. Original estimate 2-8 weeks => td 336-1344 h => mu 0.002063 to 0.000516 /h (14 d: 0.693147/336=0.002063; 56 d: 0.693147/1344=0.000516). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]
